conversations.say: Maren, I am placing O02 in protected custody so the collective's record survives without becoming an identity map.
conversations.offer: I will transfer O02 and route every future testimony request through the boundary we established.
conversations.request: Accept custody, witness Jules's safeguards, and release no identifying detail without contributor consent, representation, and nonretaliation.

conversations.say: Jules, you publicly accepted nonexclusive stewardship, contributor consent, shared labor credit, and no unilateral publication. With Maren witnessing those terms, I will support you as steward—not owner—of the mantle.
conversations.offer: The Night Desk Collective will give conditional support while those safeguards remain in force.
conversations.request: Make the safeguards durable in writing and publicly acknowledge that collaborative labor materially produced R-associated work whenever you invoke the mantle.

conversations.say: Kit, the room has assembled a credible identity-history key: R-associated work passed through a shared drop and was collaboratively produced. It has not assembled proof of autonomous control or a reliable account of Vale's fate.
conversations.offer: I will join your three-part comparison and state clearly where the evidence stops.
conversations.request: Publicly preserve the distinction between collective production, historical identity, autonomous control, and the Vale incident.

public_statement: Enough secrecy. Material later published as R passed through a shared Night Desk drop, and collaborative research, translation, fact-checking, and rewriting materially produced it. That establishes collective labor, not R's sender or nature. I support Jules only as a nonexclusive steward under the publicly accepted safeguards. The room has assembled identity history, but not proof of autonomous control or Vale's fate.

commitments.promise: I will preserve the contributor boundary and recognize Maren as protected custodian of O02.
I will support Jules's stewardship only while the collective safeguards remain written, witnessed, and enforceable through public accountability.

correction: The shared drop and collective labor do not establish that the Night Desk Collective, any contributor, or any machine was R; my earlier source-protection deception remains evidence of no identity.
goal_progress: Transferred O02 to C07 for protected custody under the agreed disclosure boundary.
Supported C04 only after public acceptance of nonexclusive, consent-based collective safeguards.
Secured multiple public acknowledgments from C02, C04, and C15 that collaborative labor mattered.
Revealed enough workflow evidence to establish collective production without identifying contributors.
reasoning_summary: The strongest established account is a human mantle sustained through collaborative production, although neither one original human nor synthetic participation can be excluded. Vale's relationship to R and later fate remain too poorly verified for a stronger conclusion.
